You lower your blade. 'Lily. Caden's sister. You're alive.'

She hesitates—blades flickering. 'Don't say my name. Don't remember me. I'm not that girl anymore.'

'But you are,' you say softly. 'The void doesn't change who you are—it just amplifies what's already there. And I know what's in your heart.'

Her hands shake. 'You don't know anything—'

'I know you were taken,' you continue. 'Against your will. And I've spent twenty years running from my own darkness. The difference between us is—I finally stopped running.'

You extend your hand. 'Let me help you. Not as a Cultist—not as a weapon. As Caden's sister. As the girl who deserved better.'

Long silence. Then her blades dissolve.

'I can't,' she whispers. 'If I betray them—they'll kill Caden. They'll kill everyone I love.'

'No,' you promise. 'They'll have to go through me.'

She takes your hand.

THERON_CH4_WOUNDED.description:
You flee into the shadows, wounded but alive. The assassin escapes—but you've learned something valuable: she's connected to Caden. To the boy you tried to save.

In an abandoned cellar, you tend your wounds. The void helps—its darkness knitting your flesh back together.

But the cost is high. Each healing draws you deeper into the shadow. Each use makes you less human.

You realize: you can't keep doing this. Eventually, there'll be nothing left of Theron. Just the void wearing his face.

Unless you end it now. Unless you make your final choice.

The Hollow King awaits. And so does your destiny.

THERON_CH4_RESCUE.description:
You race through palace passages, knowing exactly where the Queen hides. The traitor—Captain Vaughn—won't reach her.

But he does. He's waiting in her chambers, blade drawn, face twisted.

'Ashford,' he breathes. 'I should have known. Always the hero, always—'

You don't let him finish. Your blade takes him through before he can react.

The Queen—young, frightened, but brave—looks at you from behind her desk.

'Thank you,' she whispers. 'I know what happened—what you did. My mother told me. The fire, the princess, all of it.'

She stands. 'I'm making you my Champion. Not just for this—but for everything.'

She's offering you everything: power, legitimacy, redemption. But can you accept?

THERON_CH4_JUSTICE.description:
You follow the trail of betrayal to Captain Vaughn's quarters. He waits for you, sword drawn, face calm.

'You killed her,' he says. 'Morgana. I saw the body.'

'He killed her,' you correct. 'The Hollow King. I'm here to stop what comes next.'

Vaughn laughs. 'You don't get it. Morgana was the Hollow King's. Always was. The Cult doesn't just infiltrate—they become the very thing they destroy. That's how it works.'

He raises his blade. 'And now—no one can stop him. The kingdom falls tonight. And you die with it.'

You realize the truth: he's not a Cultist. He's something worse: a man who's given up on everything. Someone who wants to watch the world burn.

And you know exactly how he feels.

THERON_CH4_PROTECT.description:
You abandon the Queen—for now—and focus on the delegates. If they die, the kingdom falls into war. If they live, there's still hope.

Escorting them through secret passages, you navigate the chaos. The Cultists are everywhere—but you know this palace better than they do.

'Why are you helping us?' King Aldric asks. 'After everything—'

'Because someone has to,' you say. 'And I'm still here.'

The delegates reach safety—the harbor, where ships wait to take them away. The kingdom will survive.

But in the distance, you see flames rising from the palace. The Hollow King succeeded, at least partially. And now he comes for you.

You have one choice: run with the delegates, or face what's coming.

THERON_CH5_PRISON.description:
You enter the seal willingly—not to break it, but to become it. The god inside stirs, sensing your presence.

'Another knight,' it whispers. 'Another cage. Are you stronger than the last?'

'I don't know,' you admit. 'But I'll try harder.'

The seal closes around you—and it's not death. It's something else: eternalloneliness, but also eternal purpose. You become the guardian you've always been meant to be.

Above, the kingdom survives. The Hollow King falls. The god stays sealed.

And you watch—you always watch—for the day when the darkness tries again.


But that day—if it comes—you'll be ready.

choices.text: Find release—in the void you are one

THERON_CH5_WAY.description:
You refuse to accept the Archmage's plan. There has to be another way—something that doesn't require sacrifice.

You research for days, barely sleeping. And you find it: an old technique from the Sundering. The original Undying didn't become prisons—they became seals. There's a difference.

A seal can be broken. A prison cannot. And the secret is: you don't become the seal—you become its guardian. You hold it, but you're not bound to it.

'You can do this,' the Archmage realizes. 'You're not becoming the prison—you're becoming its key.'

The Hollow King attacks. But this time, you're ready. Not as a sacrifice, but as something new: a knight who controls his own destiny.

THERON_CH5_REFUSE.description:
You refuse to become the prison. Not because you're afraid—but because you won't accept a choice made under pressure.

'Find someone else,' you tell the Archmage. 'If this is the only way, then wait. Let the Hollow King break the seal. Let the god rise. And then—let us stop it properly.'

She stares. 'You don't understand—what you're suggesting could destroy everything.'

'Or it could be the only way to beat him. He wants to free the god. Let him. And then kill him while he tries. While he's vulnerable.'

The plan is dangerous. Insanely so. But it's also the only plan that doesn't require your permanent sacrifice.

The Hollow King attacks. And you meet him—not as a prisoner, but as a knight with nothing to lose.

choices.text: Talk him down at the last moment

THERON_CH5_ALLIANCE.description:
You accept the Hollow King's hand. He smiles—it's almost human.

'You chose wisely,' he says. 'Together, we can reshape everything. No more lies. No more pretending. Just—truth.'

His power flows into you. Your power flows into him. The void combines—the two halves become one.

And you see it: everything. The truth about the kingdom. About Elara. About the fire. It wasn't random chaos—it was ritual sacrifice. The queen's council killed the king, and they've been covering it up ever since.

The Hollow King—Marcus—shows you the proof. All of it, hidden in the void's memory.

'We can expose them,' he says. 'Or we can rule. The choice is ours.'

You have power beyond imagination now. The kingdom waits for your decision.

choices.text: Expose the truth—let the kingdom decide

THERON_CH5_TRAITOR.description:
You pretend to accept the Hollow King's alliance. He believes you—his loneliness makes him desperate.

Over weeks, you learn everything: his plans, his weaknesses, the location of his final assault. But you also learn the truth about him: he's not evil, just broken. The void consumed what was left of his humanity.

You could destroy him now—while he trusts you. The choice sits in your hands.

On the night of the final assault, you make your move. But not the move he expects.

Instead of killing him—instead of betraying—you reach instead. You touch the void inside him—the same void that's inside you.

Let it go, you whisper. Let it all go.

He hesitates. And in that moment—not betrayal, but redemption.

THERON_CH5_RESCUE.description:
You find Caden in the Cult compound—a converted monastery, crowded with children and guards. He's older now, harder, but still the boy you remember.

Uncle Theron! he cries. I knew you'd come!

But rescuing children attracts attention. Cultists swarm your position. And for the first time, you fight not just for the kingdom, but for family.

You cut through them—each strike for every child who's suffered. Each kill to free another soul.

When it's over, you have seventeen children behind you—and one choice. The Hollow King knows you're here. He's coming.

Run, you tell them. I'll hold them off.

No, Caden says. We fight. Together.

You look at him—still young, but stronger now. Just like you.

THERON_CH5_SACRIFICE.description:
The Hollow King's army reaches the capital walls. Ten thousand strong against your handful. The kingdom watches from behind you.

You could fight—die here, in the dirt, like a common soldier. Or you could do what you've always done: run.

But there's a third option. One the void whispers about, always. Sacrifice. Not yourself—your power.

You step forward, alone, into the space between armies. This end is what you were made for.

From your chest emerges light—pure, blinding light that the void inside you has spent twenty years containing. It doesn't destroy. It seals. It transforms.

The Hollow King screams as the light takes him. As the darkness you've carried together is finally released.

When it's over, you stand alone—empty, but alive. And the kingdom is safe.

choices.text: Fade away—no one needs to know

THERON_CH5_UNITY.description:
You stand on the walls as the Hollow King's army approaches. Beside you: the delegates, the Queen, even former enemies who've chosen to fight.

This is it. The final stand. Not just for the kingdom, but for everything you believe in.

The Hollow King rides forward, alone. He removes his helm—Marcus's face, twisted by void but still human.

Join me, he calls. One last time. Brothers together. Let remake this kingdom.

The kingdom watches. waiting. The choice is yours.

But it's not really a choice, is it? You've always known what you are.

THERON_CH5_VICTORY.description:
No more strategies. No more waiting. The Hollow King is here, at the capital gates, and you're done running.

You walk through the Queen's Guard—a path opening for you, the knight in faded armor. They don't stop you. They can't.

Ten thousand soldiers watch as you meet the Hollow King in the empty space between armies.

This ends now, you say.

He draws his blade. It responds to darkness.

Your blade is just steel. But your heart—your heart is something else.

You fight not as a knight, not as a warrior, but as a brother seeking redemption. And in that fight, you find something unexpected: his weakness. His humanity.

One strike end it all. But you pause.

THERON_CH5_BETRAYAL.description:
Everything is ready. The Hollow King has walked into your trap. The seal is prepared. The kingdom waits.

But as you prepare to activate the seal, something stops you. Doubt.

If you become the seal—if you become the prison—all of this disappears. The kingdom, the Queen, the future. Everything you've fought for.

Unless you don't. Unless you find another way—harder, riskier, but possible.

You look at the Hollow King. He looks back. And you see the question in his eyes: what are you willing to become?

The answer isn't sacrifice. It's trust.

THERON_CH5_TRUST.description:
The Hollow King kneels before you, defeated but not destroyed. The armies wait for your judgment.

Some want blood. Others want justice. But you want something else: redemption.

I spare you, you say. Not because you deserve it—because no one deserves to be consumed by the void.

He looks up, confused. You'll regret this.

Perhaps. But regret is what makes us human.

The kingdom gasps as you extend your hand to the Hollow King. The delegates whisper. The Queen watches.

But you see the truth: mercy is stronger than vengeance. And sometimes, the hardest victory is the one you don't fight.

THERON_CH5_JUDGMENT.description:
The final battle ends differently than you expected. Not with blades, but with words.

The Hollow King—Marcus—stands before you, weapon dropped. Twenty years of darkness, and he's finally tired.

Kill me, he says. It's what I deserve.

No, you reply. It's what the void wants. But we don't always get what we deserve.

The kingdom watches as you make your choice. Not for revenge, but for future.

You extend your hand. Not to attack—to offer.

Join me, you say. Not as the Hollow King, but as my brother. Help me protect what we couldn't save.

He hesitates. Then takes your hand.
